jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Episode opening with Homer's signature exclamation
jokes.punchline: The iconic Homer catchphrase establishing character and tone
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - high character integration and economy, but not original at this point in the series

jokes.text: If you ask me, Muhammad Ali in his prime was much better than anti-lock brakes. Yeah. But what about Johnny Mathis versus Diet Pepsi?
jokes.setup: Men having a philosophical bar discussion
jokes.punchline: They're comparing completely unrelated things as if it's a serious debate
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist humor that captures the random nature of bar conversations

jokes.text: I cannot listen to this again!
jokes.setup: Moe's exasperation at the ridiculous conversation
jokes.punchline: His deadpan reaction to the absurd comparisons
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction that grounds the absurdity

jokes.text: A meditative lily pond with big, beautiful fish that fry up really good
jokes.setup: Homer describing a romantic koi pond gift
jokes.punchline: He immediately undermines the romance by thinking about eating the fish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - romantic gesture immediately undercut by his base instincts

jokes.text: You don't even have to feed the fish, 'cause squirrels drown in it
jokes.setup: Practical benefits of having a koi pond
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions animal death as a selling point
jokes.explanation: Dark humor delivered casually, good economy and originality

jokes.text: Put a mini-beret on your wang
jokes.setup: Homer giving marriage advice
jokes.punchline: Completely absurd and inappropriate suggestion delivered as wisdom
jokes.explanation: Memorably absurd line that became quotable - high originality and economy

jokes.text: This'll really help with my speed dating. I got 400 no's
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy thinking Homer's advice will help his dating
jokes.punchline: Reveals his spectacular failure rate
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor - Comic Book Guy's social awkwardness

jokes.text: How much money did he piss away on this?
jokes.setup: Marge enjoying the peaceful koi pond and having quiet thoughts
jokes.punchline: Her first 'peaceful thought' is worry about money
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic subversion - her 'peaceful thoughts' are exactly what you'd expect from Marge

jokes.text: That caterpillar is screaming
jokes.setup: Strange noise interrupting the peaceful moment
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact identification of an impossible situation
jokes.explanation: Surreal premise delivered deadpan - high originality

jokes.text: What he needs is a visit from kindly old Doctor Foot
jokes.setup: Homer wanting to help the suffering caterpillar
jokes.punchline: His solution is to stomp on it, given a gentle medical euphemism
jokes.explanation: Clever euphemism for violence, good character integration with Homer's simple solutions

jokes.text: You're about to kill an endangered species, the screamapillar
jokes.setup: Official intervention to stop Homer
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous name 'screamapillar' for an endangered species
jokes.explanation: Creative portmanteau word that's inherently funny

jokes.text: Reversal of Freedoms Act of 1994
jokes.setup: Official citing environmental law
jokes.punchline: Satirical name suggesting government overreach
jokes.explanation: Satirical government naming - competent but not exceptional

jokes.text: Without constant reassurance, it will die. It's sexually attracted to fire
jokes.setup: Homer reading care instructions for the screamapillar
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous requirements, ending with bizarre sexual detail
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurdity with unexpected sexual element

jokes.text: Are you sure God doesn't want it to be dead? Hey, what's God gonna do? Make my wife leave me again?
jokes.setup: Homer questioning divine will about the screamapillar
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to past marital problems as if challenging God
jokes.explanation: Good callback to series continuity with dark humor about marriage

jokes.text: If he wants to sleep with us, forget it!
jokes.setup: The screamapillar apparently needs constant attention
jokes.punchline: Marge draws a firm line at bedroom boundaries
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's practical limits

jokes.text: Once upon a time there were three bears. The end
jokes.setup: Homer reading bedtime story to the screamapillar
jokes.punchline: Extremely abbreviated version of Goldilocks
jokes.explanation: Good economy in showing Homer's lazy parenting extended to bug care

jokes.text: Oh, no! I crushed that horrible bug! What should I do?
jokes.setup: Homer accidentally kills the screamapillar
jokes.punchline: Calls it 'horrible' while expressing concern about killing it
jokes.explanation: Good character contradiction - Homer's conflicted feelings

jokes.text: Bury it, quickly, before anyone finds out
jokes.setup: Homer asking what to do about the dead bug
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately suggests covering up the crime
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment - immediately thinking like a criminal

jokes.text: I'm gonna tell! The hell you will!
jokes.setup: Lisa threatening to report the dead bug
jokes.punchline: Bart's aggressive response to his sister
jokes.explanation: Standard sibling conflict but fits characters well

jokes.text: Now we're in it together. There's no going back
jokes.setup: Bart involving Lisa in the cover-up
jokes.punchline: Treating bug disposal like a serious criminal conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the crime drama parody

jokes.text: Then why don't I hear any sleep screams?
jokes.setup: Homer claiming the screamapillar is sleeping
jokes.punchline: Agent expects even sleep to be accompanied by screaming
jokes.explanation: Good logical extension of the absurd premise with economy

jokes.text: Homer Simpson, for attempted insecticide and aggravated buggery
jokes.setup: Formal legal charges being read
jokes.punchline: 'Buggery' as a pun for crimes against bugs
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels

jokes.text: Duffman's pension has been mismanaged! Oh, yeah!
jokes.setup: Legal case about pension mismanagement
jokes.punchline: Duffman maintains his enthusiastic catchphrase despite serious financial problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Duffman's inability to break character even in crisis

jokes.text: That party-hearty attitude is a registered trademark of the Duff Corporation
jokes.setup: Legal objection to Duffman's behavior
jokes.punchline: His personality is literally corporate property
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate control over mascot characters

jokes.text: Lousy community service! Meals on Wheels! Eat it up or I go to jail
jokes.setup: Homer doing community service
jokes.punchline: Threatening elderly people to eat their meals
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's selfishness even in service

jokes.text: You smell like cobbler. Now let's not get into who smells like what
jokes.setup: Discussion about discontinued cobbler
jokes.punchline: Homer deflecting the smell observation awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Mild awkward humor showing Homer's discomfort

jokes.text: Please don't kill me! I won't tell anyone about the skeleton!
jokes.setup: Homer panicking at Mrs. Bellamy's house
jokes.punchline: Immediately mentions incriminating evidence while begging for mercy
jokes.explanation: Good panic comedy showing Homer making things worse

jokes.text: And I could bring you more victims! Like Lenny! He'd go great with wild rice!
jokes.setup: Homer continuing his panic
jokes.punchline: Offering to help with cannibalism and menu planning
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with good escalation and character-specific food obsession

jokes.text: Oh, don't be such a nervous Pervis
jokes.setup: Mrs. Bellamy responding to Homer's panic
jokes.punchline: Made-up rhyming phrase to dismiss his fears
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay typical of elderly character speech patterns

jokes.text: Oh, Homer, I feel like I'm talking to Bennett Cerf
jokes.setup: Homer telling a boring story about a SuperBall
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to a famous wit/wordsmith ironically
jokes.explanation: Dated reference that may not land with all audiences

jokes.text: Yeah, I've gotten a lot of compliments about my talking
jokes.setup: Mrs. Bellamy's sarcastic compliment
jokes.punchline: Homer completely missing the sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness to sarcasm

jokes.text: And I can ride my bike real fast
jokes.setup: Mrs. Bellamy complimenting Homer's strength and looks
jokes.punchline: Homer adds a childish non-sequitur to the compliments
jokes.explanation: Good childlike character moment for Homer

jokes.text: Hey! I'm not running an employment service, you old... Oh, you mean me? I'd be delighted
jokes.setup: Mrs. Bellamy asking for a muscular he-man
jokes.punchline: Homer starts to object before realizing she means him
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Homer's ego overriding his annoyance

jokes.text: Hey! I'm not running an employment service, you old... Oh, you mean me? I'd be delighted
jokes.setup: Mrs. Bellamy asking for a muscular he-man
jokes.punchline: Homer's initial offense turning to ego boost
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Homer's vanity overriding his annoyance

jokes.text: Marge, I can't say no to a helpless old lady. They put spells on you!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he keeps helping Mrs. Bellamy
jokes.punchline: Genuinely believes old ladies have magical powers
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic and superstition

jokes.text: All I have left of my Chester is his tattered old Army jacket
jokes.setup: Mrs. Bellamy appearing helpless
jokes.punchline: Immediately manipulating Marge's sympathy
jokes.explanation: Standard manipulation tactic but sets up character dynamics

jokes.text: Darn her socks? I say darn her!
jokes.setup: Mrs. Bellamy asking Marge to darn socks
jokes.punchline: Marge uses 'darn' as both literal task and mild curse
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that fits Marge's mild-mannered rebelliousness

jokes.text: Oh! She would fall there!
jokes.setup: News that Gertie fell outside the thrift store
jokes.punchline: Elderly woman's catty response about appropriate fall location
jokes.explanation: Good elderly cattiness and social judgment

jokes.text: Hey, who do I have to gum to get a refill?
jokes.setup: Elderly woman wanting drink refill
jokes.punchline: Age-appropriate variation of crude expression
jokes.explanation: Clever age-appropriate adaptation of vulgar phrase

jokes.text: Don't you read enough at school, bookworm?
jokes.setup: Child asking to read while waiting
jokes.punchline: Elderly woman treating reading as negative
jokes.explanation: Standard generational conflict humor

jokes.text: The Missus prefers you call me Simpson
jokes.setup: Marge complaining about Mrs. Bellamy
jokes.punchline: Homer adopting formal servant language
jokes.explanation: Good class dynamics and Homer's adaptation to servitude

jokes.text: So it's a good thing she died, from our point of view, financially
jokes.setup: Learning the Simpsons inherited $50,000
jokes.punchline: Homer's callous financial perspective on murder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer callousness in the face of tragedy

jokes.text: Did you do it? Chief Wiggum! Homer and I are innocent! I'm sorry, Marge. I can't believe I tried to trick you with such an... Did you do it? No!
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum's interrogation technique
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning subtlety after one attempt
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum incompetence showing his lack of interrogation skills

jokes.text: Does that ever work? No. No, never does
jokes.setup: Homer asking about Wiggum's technique
jokes.punchline: Wiggum admitting his method never works
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency with Wiggum's incompetence

jokes.text: Yeah, sure. Go ahead. I'll be back on the streets by dinnertime. You'll see
jokes.setup: Being arrested for murder
jokes.punchline: Homer's overconfident prediction about quick release
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony given the severity of murder charges

jokes.text: Yeah. If Dad killed everyone he talked about killing, would any of us be here? You'd be dead a million times
jokes.setup: Discussion of whether Homer could be a killer
jokes.punchline: Lisa pointing out Homer's frequent death threats to family
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor referencing ongoing series gag about family violence

jokes.text: Well, we've all got that voice in our heads telling us to kill. You just have to drown it out. I've been working on the railroad all the livelong day
jokes.setup: Discussing murderous impulses
jokes.punchline: Moe casually admitting homicidal thoughts and his coping mechanism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character moment - dark psychology delivered matter-of-factly with specific coping strategy

jokes.text: Oh, man. What a day! I'd kill for a beer. Right away, sir. I don't want no trouble
jokes.setup: Homer using casual expression about wanting beer
jokes.punchline: Moe taking the threat literally given murder suspicions
jokes.explanation: Good situational humor playing on common expressions

jokes.text: I'd stab somebody for a pickle. Give me some peanuts. You didn't say you'd kill me. I'll kill you if you don't give me some peanuts
jokes.setup: Pattern of Homer making casual death threats
jokes.punchline: Moe's literal interpretation requiring specific threats
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the absurd requirements of literal interpretation

jokes.text: Next on the Springfield Death Tour is the home of Marge and Homer Simpson, also known as 'H-Diddy and his Murder Ho.'
jokes.setup: Tourist bus visiting murder scene
jokes.punchline: Hip-hop style nicknames for suspected murderers
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture parody with creative nickname adaptation

jokes.text: Go away! Wait, wait! That's a suggestion, not a threat
jokes.setup: Marge yelling at tour bus
jokes.punchline: Homer clarifying they're not threatening anyone
jokes.explanation: Good situational humor about careful word choice

jokes.text: You've been through my delicates, my silkies, my dainties, and my unmentionables!
jokes.setup: Marge complaining about police search
jokes.punchline: Increasingly euphemistic terms for underwear
jokes.explanation: Good Marge prudishness with escalating euphemisms

jokes.text: Because I can just swipe it later from the evidence locker!
jokes.setup: Wiggum rejecting Homer's bribe offer
jokes.punchline: Revealing his plan to steal it anyway
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Wiggum's casual corruption

jokes.text: You know, Chief, if you let us go, there's a diamond necklace in it for you. I hope you're not suggesting that I would take that necklace as a bribe. Think again, dirtbag. Because I can just swipe it later from the evidence locker!
jokes.setup: Homer attempting to bribe Wiggum
jokes.punchline: Wiggum rejecting bribery but planning theft instead
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing Wiggum's twisted ethics

jokes.text: Don't worry, Marge. I'll cut us a deal by becoming a jailhouse snitch. I know who stopped up the toilet
jokes.setup: Homer planning to help their legal situation
jokes.punchline: His valuable information is about bathroom problems
jokes.explanation: Good Homer misunderstanding of valuable prison information

jokes.text: From now on, you're Dingus Squatford Jr. and Pamela E. Lee
jokes.setup: Renaming the Simpson children
jokes.punchline: The absurd hillbilly name and celebrity reference
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between ridiculous and pop culture names

jokes.text: Young'uns, meet your new brother and sister. They's worth five dollars a day county money. I'm Bart. And this is Lisa. Them's city names. From now on, you're Dingus Squatford Jr. and Pamela E. Lee
jokes.setup: Bart and Lisa placed with foster family
jokes.punchline: Rural family's financial motivation and name changes
jokes.explanation: Good social satire about foster care system and rural stereotypes

jokes.text: Verdict? Is that what we were supposed to do? Well, in all my years on the bench... 'Cause that's what we did!
jokes.setup: Judge asking for jury verdict
jokes.punchline: Jury uncertain about their basic function but claiming they did it
jokes.explanation: Good satire of jury incompetence

jokes.text: Can't you do anything? Surprise witnesses? Evidence tampering? Play the race card! Play it!
jokes.setup: Homer begging his lawyer for help
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate and unethical suggestions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of desperate legal strategies

jokes.text: Well, then I hope you enjoy your stay in hell
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge saying they're not Catholic
jokes.punchline: The priest's vindictive response
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing religious pettiness

jokes.text: Oh, go home and have sex with your wife! That's it. Bring it on!
jokes.setup: Priest suggesting Homer will go to hell
jokes.punchline: Homer's inappropriate comeback leading to priest fight
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's defiance even facing death

jokes.text: This cannon is gonna be full when I go off. Ka-boom!
jokes.setup: Homer planning surprise for his execution
jokes.punchline: Planning to defecate in the electric chair as final act
jokes.explanation: Dark scatological humor fitting Homer's crude defiance

jokes.text: Just think of them as twinkling stars
jokes.setup: Marge being disturbed by other inmates watching them
jokes.punchline: Homer's romantic reframing of the voyeuristic situation
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Homer's optimism in dark circumstances

jokes.text: I want to confess. I killed and robbed the old lady all by myself. Marge is completely innocent. The only thing she's guilty of is loving too much
jokes.setup: Homer confessing to save Marge
jokes.punchline: Romantic description of her only 'crime'
jokes.explanation: Good romantic gesture with typical Homer sentimentality

jokes.text: The only thing she's guilty of is loving too much
jokes.setup: Homer confessing to save Marge
jokes.punchline: The melodramatic soap opera language
jokes.explanation: Decent parody of melodramatic tropes

jokes.text: Give me your hands, boss
jokes.setup: Homer walking to execution
jokes.punchline: Reference to The Green Mile
jokes.explanation: Basic movie reference, not particularly clever

jokes.text: The governor says he hopes you're a twitcher!
jokes.setup: Apparent call from governor about Homer's execution
jokes.punchline: Governor hoping for entertaining death throes rather than clemency
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor subverting expectation of gubernatorial intervention

jokes.text: You're on Frame Up! That's right. Homer Simpson, you're the latest victim of the new reality show, Frame Up!
jokes.setup: Homer about to be executed for murder
jokes.punchline: Entire murder case was elaborate reality TV prank
jokes.explanation: Major plot twist that recontextualizes entire episode - high structural impact

jokes.text: There never was a Mrs. Bellamy. Only me, Carmen Electra!
jokes.setup: Revealing the murder victim was fake
jokes.punchline: Celebrity cameo as the 'victim'
jokes.explanation: Celebrity stunt casting with mild surprise value

jokes.text: Frame Up is Fox's latest hit, right after No Pants Island and Fart Date!
jokes.setup: Explaining the reality show concept
jokes.punchline: Satirical fake Fox reality show names
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor satirizing Fox's own reality programming

jokes.text: You tied up the judicial system, costing the city millions of dollars, just for a TV show? Yes. And I'm gonna be in the show? Yes! Can Eddy and Lou have producer credits? Yup!
jokes.setup: Wiggum questioning the ethics of the fake trial
jokes.punchline: Immediately shifting to personal benefit concerns
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum character moment - corruption disguised as career opportunity

jokes.text: We were in the greenroom. I had so much shrimp
jokes.setup: Kids explaining where they were during parents' ordeal
jokes.punchline: Lisa's focus on catering rather than trauma
jokes.explanation: Good child's perspective prioritizing food over family crisis

jokes.text: Uh, Homer. My face is up here. I've made my choice
jokes.setup: Carmen Electra talking to Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer openly ogling her body despite wife being present
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer inappropriateness with visual comedy element
